rows.notes: Page title at top. Lettering is legible though slightly abbreviated (Recepta instead of Receipts); no monetary figures on this line.
Pounds '60' is clearly aligned in the pounds column. Shillings/pence positions are blank. Description spelling slightly uncertain ('Prect.' abbreviation) but identifiable.

Parish name handwriting is ambiguous (reads 'Gavi near'/'Gavinear'); a clear '60' appears in the pounds column aligned with this row but the original is somewhat faint. Chose 60 for pounds; shillings/pence blank.
Pounds '119' and shillings '5' are reasonably clear and well aligned to the pounds/shillings columns. Description 'Candlemass Aug.' is legible enough to transcribe.
Reads '2 15 0' in the amount columns (pounds 2, shillings 15). Description begins with 'Do' (ditto) and 'Braun & Capon' is reasonably legible.
The parish/clerk name is faint ('Monkninnet' or similar) but there is a clear '20' in the pounds column aligned with this row. Shillings/pence blank.
Amount reads clearly as '7 5 9' (pounds 7, 5 shillings, 9 pence). Description 'Kidlington Vic.' is legible.
Clear amounts '3 0 0' aligned to this row. Description reads as a ditto line 'Do Mills' with the abbreviation 'pro Dim. an.'

rows.description: Setheran for Divis Ann ad Test. Anno
rows.notes: Amount reads '5 19 8' fairly clearly. The description contains abbreviations and is harder to parse exactly; transcribed to reflect visible letters ('Setheran for Divis Ann').
Amount reads '4 11 8 3/4' — the pence column clearly shows '8' plus a small '3/4' loop sign; interpreted as 8 pence + 3/4 (0.75). Description 'Taylor & Wife pro Can.' is legible.
Amount reads '0 16 1' (16 shillings, 1 penny) aligned clearly. Pounds column has a small '0'. Description legible.
Amount '2 14 0' is clear and well aligned. Description has two names separated by a slash; reading is confident.
Amount '2 14 0' clearly visible. Name 'Richard Hains' legible; 'pro Ann.' as in other rows.
Amount '2 14 0' repeated and clear. Name transcribed as 'Richard Judge'; neat alignment with previous annuity entries.
Underlined subtotal for the block. Reads '301 11 2 3/4' — pounds and shillings well aligned; pence shows '2' with small '3/4' mark interpreted as 0.75.
Reads '50 0 0' clearly in its own line. Heading 'Exchequer Annuity' is legible (spelling 'Exchequer').
Amount '63 8 4' is clear and aligned. Description reads 'Interest of Stock in Fund' with minor abbreviation for 'Fund' but unambiguous.
Subtotal under the two preceding rows; reads '113 8 4' clearly and is underlined, confirming it is a calculated total.
Section header for miscellaneous receipts. Handwriting is legible though slightly stylized (Varia).

rows.description: of Mr Bowles 2 years Rent for Longworth ???
rows.notes: Amount '175 0 0' is clear. Description reads 'of Mr Bowles 2 years Rent for Longworth' though place name trailing letters are faint — transcribed with ??? to indicate uncertainty in the place name.

rows.description: of Mr Shawles payment of D. Stockings(?) Receipt
rows.notes: Clear amount '110 0 0'. The description is badly abbreviated and partly crossed/inked; I read 'Shawles' (or 'Shawles'/'Sharles') and 'payment of' followed by a name that looks like 'D. Stockings' but is uncertain.
Amount '10 0 0' clear. Description reads 'W Mr Martins Plate Money' — likely 'Wm' abbreviation for William; wording legible.

rows.description: of Mr Bowles 2 years Rent for Longwith to Michael ???
rows.notes: Amount '175 0 0' is clear. Description repeats a Bowles rent receipt line; trailing words (possibly recipient or place) are faint and partially overwritten.

rows.description: of Wm J. C. Huxley Esqr. payment of Stockings Receipt
rows.notes: Amount '100 0 0' clear. The name is read as 'Wm J. C. Huxley Esqr.' though some letters are cramped; the phrase 'payment' and 'Receipt' are visible but abbreviated.

rows.description: of Mr Richard's Deed, Fees to the ???
rows.notes: Amount '17 0 0' is clear. The description is cramped and partially crossed; I read 'of Mr Richard's Deed fees & the [house?]' but the destination word is uncertain.

rows.description: Morton Estate from Mich. 1702 to Mich. 1703 - Money Rent
rows.notes: Amount '265 0 0' is clearly written. Description 'Morton Estate from Mich 1702 to Mich 1703' is legible and indicates a rent period; 'Money Rent' legible.
Short line under the Morton Estate amounts reads '6-10-0' clearly. The preceding short description is abbreviated ('Cor De' or similar) and uncertain; amount transcription is confident.
Subtotal beneath the Morton Estate block reads '271-10-0' (pounds 271, shillings 10). The underline and position make this clearly a calculated total for the small group.

rows.description: Allowd Disbursmts - Land Tax at 4 per ct.
rows.notes: Amount '39-3-0' is legible. Description 'Allowd Disbursmts' and 'Land Tax at 4 per ct' is readable though abbreviated.
Amount reads as '16-7-10 1/2' — pounds 16, 7 shillings, 10 pence with a halfpenny indicated. The fraction mark for the halfpenny is faint but consistent with the small loop 'ob' conventions.
Subtotal line (sum of disbursements) reads '49-10-10 1/2' — amounts are aligned and the halfpenny mark is present but moderately faint.
Line showing remaining balance reads '222-9-0' clearly and is underlined/boxed. Description is abbreviated 'Remr. &c.' (remainder etc.).
A repeat of the carried figure '222 9 0' appears again on the right margin; the alignment and numerals are clear — likely the column total being brought down.
Final bottom-right total reads '829 9 0'. Numerals are readable though slightly slanted; likely the page grand total. Placement at page foot confirms role as final total.
